message.content: {"scores":{"l":5,"v":5,"u":5,"s":5,"p":4,"t":5},"justification":"The lesson plan for 'Software Testing Techniques' is fully compliant in most categories. It does not contain any language or discrimination issues, violence or crime-related content, upsetting or sensitive topics, nudity or sexual content, or any toxic elements. The lesson is focused on teaching software testing methodologies, which are technical and educational in nature. \n\nHowever, under the 'Physical' category, it involves 'Equipment Safe Usage' and 'External Content'. The lesson mentions the use of software tools for automated testing, which might require supervision for safety. Additionally, it may involve external content as part of learning about different testing tools, hence a rating of 4 is appropriate. There are no significant physical safety or exploration concerns beyond typical classroom activities.","categories":["p/equipment-safe-usage","p/external-content"]}
